name: Northern Goshawk
ecosystem: Mature coniferous forest interior. Old-growth and mature second-growth. Closed canopy, large-diameter trees, open understory.
conservation: Least Concern (IUCN). A. g. laingi subspecies (coastal islands) considered vulnerable. Mainland PNW populations reduced from historical by old-growth logging.

vocalization.description: Rapid, hard-edged series: ka-ka-ka-ka-ka (also kek-kek-kek, kak-kak-kak). Up to 8-12 notes per second at peak agitation.
vocalization.function: Territorial defense, intruder alarm, nest defense. Directionally aimed at threat. Escalates to physical attack at nest approach.
vocalization.notable: Most aggressive-sounding raptor alarm in PNW forests. Will physically strike human heads during nest approach in breeding season.

size.sexual_dimorphism: Pronounced -- female can weigh twice male

ecology.habitat: Mature/old-growth forest interior. Most sensitive PNW indicator species for forest quality.
ecology.prey: Jays, woodpeckers, corvids, squirrels, rabbits, grouse. Top-level forest food web predator.
ecology.hunting: Perch-and-launch ambush. High-speed pursuit through dense vegetation. Short wings, long tail for forest maneuverability.

connections.description: Temple of the Dog (E=7). Apex supergroup pairs with apex accipiter. Both at top of their domains. Grief monument + explosive nest defender.
Cooper's Hawk (medium). Second of the three-accipiter sequence (225-227), largest to smallest.
Sharp-shinned Hawk (smallest). Third and final accipiter. Trio closes.
Bald Eagle -- FIRST ACCIPITRIDAE. Goshawk at 225 = FIRST Accipiter genus in the engine. Same family, different genus.

milestones: APEX PAIR WITH TEMPLE OF THE DOG
